mag- -an.function: Actor trigger/focus verb circumfix used to express an action done reciprocally or simultaneously. halik (“kiss”) + mag- -an → maghalikan (“to kiss each other”) kanta (“song”) + mag- -han → magkantahan (“to sing together; to sing one after another”) tulong (“help”) + mag- -an → magtulungan (“to help each other”)

ka- -han.function: alternative form of ka- -an; Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. In some cases, phoneme change can occur and /h/ becomes /n/. saya + ka- -han → kasiyahan dami + ka- -han → karamihan gusto + ka- -han → kagustuhan bihasa + ka- -han → kabihasnan

ma- -an.function: to suffer the quality of adjective indicated dali (“easiness”) + ma- -an → madalian (“to have no difficulty”) hirap (“difficulty”) + ma- -an → mahirapan (“to have difficulty”); to attribute certain quality to something ganda (“beauty”) + ma- -an → magandahan (“to find something beautiful”) pangit (“ugliness”) + ma- -an → mapangitan (“to find something ugly”) laki (“size”) + ma- -an → malakihan (“to find something big”) liit (“smallness”) + ma- -an → maliitan (“to find something small”) haba (“length”) + ma- -an → mahabaan (“to find something long”) iksi (“shortness”) + ma- -an → maiksian (“to find something short”) bigat (“heaviness”) + ma- -an → mabigatan (“to find something heavy”) gaan (“lightness”) + ma- -an → magaanan (“to find something light in weight”)

ka- -nan.function: alternative form of ka- -an pakana (“scheme”) + ka- -nan → kapakanan (“sake”) sala (“fault”) + ka- -nan → kasalanan (“sin”) bihasa (“accustomed”) + ka- -nan → kabihasnan (“civilization”) kaya (“competence”) + ka- -nan → kakayanan (“ability”); Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. In some cases, phoneme change can occur and /h/ becomes /n/. sala + ka- -nan → kasalanan; Sometimes, the final vowel of the root word disappears when the suffix is added. pakana + ka- -nan → kapakanan bihasa + ka- -nan → kabihasnan

ma- -in.function: having a certain quality to a high degree tulong (“help”) → matulungin (“helpful”) inip (“boredom, impatience”) → mainipin (“inclined to be bored or impatient”)

dalub- -an.function: to form a study of a particular subject: -logy, -nomy. lason (“poison”) → dalublasunan (“toxicology”) tala (“star”) → dalubtalaan (“astronomy”) lumot (“moss”) → dalublumutan (“phycology”)

ka- -an.etymology: Inherited from Proto-Austronesian *ka- -an. Compare Malay ke- -an.
ka- -an.function: used to form abstract nouns payapa (“peaceful”) + ka- -an → kapayapaan (“peace”) malay (“awareness”) + ka- -an → kamalayan (“consciousness”); used to form nouns indicating a larger location gubat (“woods”) + ka- -an → kagubatan (“forest”) dagat (“sea”) + ka- -an → karagatan (“ocean”); used to form proper nouns indicating a domain of a people or place Tagalog (“Tagalog”) + ka- -an → Katagalugan (“Tagalog area”) Bisaya (“Visayan”) + ka- -an → Kabisayaan (“Visayas; Visayan area”) Maynila (“Manila”) + ka- -an → Kamaynilaan (“Metro Manila; Manila area”); used to form words meaning “one who receives or (-an) from someone (ka-)” ibig (“love”) + ka- -an → kaibigan (“friend; person who receives love from someone; lover”) halik (“kiss”) + ka- -an → kahalikan (“person who receives kisses from someone; kissing-mate”); Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. In some cases, phoneme change can occur by epenthesis and /h/ becomes /n/. saya + ka- -an → kasiyahan dami + ka- -an → karamihan gusto + ka- -an → kagustuhan bihasa + ka- -an → kabihasnan; Due to allophony, /d/ turns into /r/ when inserted before -an or after ka-. baliktad + ka- -an → kabaliktaran lunod + ka- -an → kanluran gawad + ka- -an → kagawaran dami + ka- -an → karamihan dangal + ka- -an → karangalan danas + ka- -an → karanasan; Sense 4: The ka- prefix has a stress while the stress of the root shifts to the penultimate syllable.; Nouns containing this affix are generally treated as non-count, collective or mass nouns, but some such words usually referring to people have been used as count nouns even in formal usage (e.g. public speeches, news reporting), such as with kalalakihan (“men; male people in general”) and kabataan (“youth; young people in general”). Such usage has been repeatedly denounced by the Commission on the Filipino Language (KWF).

ka- -an.etymology: Inherited from Proto-Austronesian *ka- -an, compare kina- -an, Tagalog ka- -an, Malay ke- -an. Compare ka-, and -an.
ka- -an.function: (added to inanimate nouns) indicates a place where such objects are found Synonyms: -an, -anan sagbot (“grass, weed”) + ka- -an → kasagbotan (“grassland”) saging (“banana”) + ka- -an → kasagingan (“banana plantation”); (added to nouns) forms collective nouns tawo (“human”) + ka- -an → katawhan (“people”) dagat (“sea”) + ka- -an → kadagatan (“seas (collectively)”) forms proper nouns indicating a domain of a people or place Bisaya (“Visayan people”) + ka- -an → Kabisay-an (“Visayas region”) forms numerals that are multiples of ten duha (“two”) + ka- -an → kaluhaan (“twenty”); forms proper nouns indicating a domain of a people or place Bisaya (“Visayan people”) + ka- -an → Kabisay-an (“Visayas region”); forms numerals that are multiples of ten duha (“two”) + ka- -an → kaluhaan (“twenty”); (added to adjectives or verbs) forms abstract nouns Synonyms: ka-, pagka-, ka- -on batan-on (“young”) + ka- -an → kabatan-onan (“youth”) husay (“orderly”) + ka- -an → kahusayan (“peace, resolution”) daog (“to win”) + ka- -an → kadaogan (“victory”); In forming abstract nouns, ka- and pagka- are more productive in colloquial language.; Placement of stress: Sense 1: Stress is placed on the last syllable (-an) ságing → kasagingán káhoy → kakahoyán The rest: Stress is usually placed on the penultimate syllable after affixation: batán-on → kabatan-ónan daóg → kadaógan; Sense 1: Stress is placed on the last syllable (-an); The rest: Stress is usually placed on the penultimate syllable after affixation:; When attached directly after a vowel without a glottal stop, an epenthetic /h/ is usually inserted between the two vowels. However, this isn't always the case, as /ʔ/ (glottal stop) may be inserted in this position instead. ulo → kaulohan, but higala → kahigalaan In a handful of cases, an epenthetic /nʔ/ is inserted instead: lalaki → kalalakin-an babaye → kababayin-an; In a handful of cases, an epenthetic /nʔ/ is inserted instead: lalaki → kalalakin-an babaye → kababayin-an; In some affixations, the vowel in the syllable before or after the stressed syllable is typically omitted, as long as it does not break the language's CVCCVC syllable structure. After which metathesis or some other sound change may happen to consonants in awkward positions. *kaʔupátan > *kaʔpátan > kapʔátan (kap-atan) *kabisáyaʔan > Kabisáyʔan (Kabisay-an) *kapupúluʔan > *kapupúlʔan > kapupúdʔan (kapupud-an) This is not universally observed; more so in colloquial, newly-derived words; This is not universally observed; more so in colloquial, newly-derived words

dalub- -han.function: alternative form of dalub- -an banwa (“state”) + dalub- -han → dalubbanwahan (“political science”); Used when the root does not end with a consonant or a glottal stop.

ma- -nan.function: alternative form of ma- -an kuha (“take”) + ma- -nan → makuhanan / makunan tuto (“learning”) + ma- -nan → matutunan kaya (“capability”) + ma- -nan → makayanan; Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. In some cases, phoneme change can occur and /h/ becomes /n/. kuha + ma- -nan → makunan / makuhanan

ma- -han.function: alternative form of ma- -an; Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop.

pa- -han.function: alternative form of pa- -an; Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. upa + pa- -han → paupahan bili + pa- -han → pamilihan laki + pa- -han → palakihan

mag- -han.function: alternative form of mag- -an; Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. saya + mag- -han → magkasiyahan kanta + mag- -han → magkantahan gusto + mag- -han → magkagustuhan

pa- -an.function: (causative) Object trigger: to have someone do something pa- -an + bukas (“open”) → pabuksan (“to have someone open something”) Pinabuksan ko kay papa ang pinto.I had dad open the door.; (causative) Object trigger suffix: indicates performance of the action of the verb on or to an object pa- -an + lagay (“put”) → palagyan (“to have someone put something somewhere”) Pinalagyan ni Maribel ng carpet ang sahig.Maribel had someone put a carpet on the floor.; Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. upa + pa- -an → paupahan laki + pa- -an → palakihan; Due to allophony, /d/ turns into /r/ when inserted before -an or after pa-. likod + pa- -an → palikuran dami + pa- -an → paramihan dangal + pa- -an → parangalan

pa- -hin.function: alternative form of pa- -in; Normally, /h/ is inserted before -in when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. In some cases, phoneme change can occur and /h/ becomes /n/. punta + pa- -hin → papuntahin ganda + pa- -hin → pagandahin laki + pa- -hin → palakihin tawa + pa- -hin → patawanin

pag- -nan.etymology:
Circumfix[edit]
pag- -nan (Baybayin spelling ᜉᜄ᜔ ᜈᜈ᜔)
alternative form of pag- -an tawa (“laugh”) + pag- -nan → pagtawanan (“to laugh at”) talo (“quarrel”) + pag- -nan → pagtalunan (“to quarrel with”)
Usage notes[edit]
Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. In some cases, phoneme change can occur and /h/ becomes /n/. sabi + pag- -nan → pagsabihan duda + pag- -nan → pagdudahan base + pag- -nan → pagbasehan tawa + pag- -nan → pagtawanan
Derived terms[edit]
Tagalog terms circumfixed with pag- -nan pagtalunan pagtatalunan pagtatawanan pagtawanan papagtalunan pinagtalunan pinagtawanan
Anagrams[edit]
pang- -an, Pangan

pa- -nin.function: alternative form of pa- -in tawa (“laugh”) + pa- -nin → patawanin (“to be made to laugh”) talo (“defeat”) + pa- -nin → patalunin (“to let one be defeated”); Normally, /h/ is inserted before -in when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. In some cases, phoneme change can occur and /h/ becomes /n/. punta + pa- -nin → papuntahin ganda + pa- -nin → pagandahin laki + pa- -nin → palakihin tawa + pa- -nin → patawanin

pa- -in.function: (causative) Object trigger - Circumfix to form the causative verb form of the adjectives tahimik (“quiet”) + pa- -in → patahimikin (“to silence”) lamig (“cold”) + pa- -in → palamigin (“to cool”) Palamigin mo itong inumin.Cool this drink.; (causative) Object trigger - Indicates performance of the action of the verb daan (“pass”) + pa- -in → paraanin (“make someone give way”) upo (“sitting”) + pa- -in → paupuin (“make someone sit down”) Paupuin mo 'ang mga bisita.Ask the visitors to sit down.; Normally, /h/ is inserted before -in when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. In some cases, phoneme change can occur and /h/ becomes /n/. punta + pa- -in → papuntahin ganda + pa- -in → pagandahin laki + pa- -in → palakihin tawa + pa- -in → patawanin; Due to allophony, /d/ turns into /r/ when inserted before -in or after pa-. tawad + pa- -in → patawarin dami + pa- -in → paramihin danas + pa- -in → paranasin

pag- -an.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
pag- -han
pag- -nan
Circumfix[edit]
pag- -an (Baybayin spelling ᜉᜄ᜔ ◌ᜈ᜔)
Object trigger - perform the action of the verb on/ at/ to/ in something or someone sabi (“say”) + pag- -an → pagsabihan (“to be reprimanded, to be warned”) Pinagsabihan ko na siya.I already warned him. higanti (“revenge”) + pag- -an → paghigantihan (“to be taken revenge on”) Pinaghigantihan niya ang pagkamatay ng lolo niya.He took revenge on the death of his grandfather. himasok (“say”) + pag- -an → panghimasukan (“to be meddled in”) Huwag mong panghimasukan ang buhay ko.Don't poke your nose in my life.
Derived terms[edit]
Tagalog terms circumfixed with pag- -an pag-aralan pag-awayan pag-initan pagbawalan paghahayupan paghinalaan pagkaitan pagkalooban pagkatiwalaan pagmalupitan pagtakpan pagtalunan pagtripan pagtutulungan papagtalunan pinagtalunan
Further reading[edit]
Fiona De Vos; Frederik De Vos ((Can we date this quote?)), “Pag-…-an 1, Pang-…-an: Tagalog Verb Affixes”, in Learning Tagalog
Anagrams[edit]
ganap, panga, panag-, Gapan

pam- -an.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
pam- -han — for roots ending in vowels, not followed by glottal stops
pan- -an — before root initials ⟨d⟩, ⟨s⟩ and ⟨t⟩, will sometimes remove initial ⟨d⟩, ⟨s⟩ and ⟨t⟩
pam- -an.function: alternative form of pang- -an for roots with initials ⟨b⟩ and ⟨p⟩, which will usually have them removed bahala (“responsibility”) + pam- -an → pamahalaan (“government”) bili (“purchase”) + pam- -an → pamilihan (“market”) puno (“leader”) + pam- -an → pamunuan (“administration”); Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. bili + pam- -an → pamilihan pito + pam- -an → pampituhan

pag- -han.etymology:
Circumfix[edit]
pag- -han (Baybayin spelling ᜉᜄ᜔ ᜑᜈ᜔)
alternative form of pag- -an
Usage notes[edit]
Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. In some cases, phoneme change can occur and /h/ becomes /n/. sabi + pag- -han → pagsabihan duda + pag- -han → pagdudahan base + pag- -han → pagbasehan tawa + pag- -han → pagtawanan
Derived terms[edit]
Tagalog terms circumfixed with pag- -han pagbabasehan pagbasehan pagdudahan paghigantihan pagsabihan pagsamantalahan pagsasamantalahan pagtaluhan pagtataluhan papagtaluhan pinagtaluhan pagsisihan

pan- -han.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
pang- -han
pam- -han — before root initials ⟨b⟩ and ⟨p⟩, will sometimes remove initial ⟨b⟩ and ⟨p⟩
pan- -han.function: alternative form of pan- -an; Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. In some cases, phoneme change can occur and /h/ becomes /n/. tira + pan- -han → panirahan talo + pan- -han → panaluhan talo + pan- -han → panalunan

pam- -han.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
pang- -han
pan- -han — before root initials ⟨d⟩, ⟨s⟩ and ⟨t⟩, will sometimes remove initial ⟨d⟩, ⟨s⟩ and ⟨t⟩
Circumfix[edit]
pam- -han (Baybayin spelling ᜉᜋ᜔ ᜑᜈ᜔)
alternative form of pam- -an
Usage notes[edit]
Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. bili + pam- -han → pamilihan
Derived terms[edit]
Tagalog terms circumfixed with pam- -han pamilihan

pan- -an.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
pan- -han — for roots ending in vowels, not followed by glottal stops
pam- -an — before root initials ⟨b⟩ and ⟨p⟩, will sometimes remove initial ⟨b⟩ and ⟨p⟩
pan- -an.function: alternative form of pang- -an for roots with initials ⟨d⟩, ⟨s⟩ and ⟨t⟩, which will usually have them removed titik (“letter”) + pan- -an → panitikan (“literature”) sandali (“a moment”) + pan- -an → panandalian (“short-term”) sagot (“response”) + pan- -an → pananagutan (“responsibility”); Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. In some cases, phoneme change can occur and /h/ becomes /n/. tira + pan- -an → panirahan talo + pan- -an → panaluhan talo + pan- -an → panalunan

sa- -an.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
sa- -han
sa- -nan
See sa- and -an.
sa- -an.function: forms terms to denote a method of doing things sa- -an + pilit (“forced”) → sapilitan (“compulsory”) sa- -an + palad (“palm; fate”) → sapalaran (“risky activity”) sa- -an + bunot (“uprooting”) → sabunutan (“a fight via pulling each other's hair”)

pang- -an.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
pam- -an — before root initials ⟨b⟩ and ⟨p⟩, will sometimes remove initial ⟨b⟩ and ⟨p⟩
pan- -an — before root initials ⟨d⟩, ⟨s⟩ and ⟨t⟩, will sometimes remove initial ⟨d⟩, ⟨s⟩ and ⟨t⟩
pang- -han — for roots ending in vowels, not followed by glottal stops
Circumfix[edit]
pang- -an (Baybayin spelling ᜉᜅ᜔ ◌ᜈ᜔)
(instrumentative circumfix) used to form nouns indicating a place or concept where the action expressed by the root is performed bili (“purchase”) + pang- -an → pamilihan (“market”) puno (“leader”) + pang- -an → pamunuan (“administration”) tunton (“guidance”) + pang- -an → panuntunan (“guideline”)
(adjectival circumfix) used to form adjectives indicating the root is intended for dalawa (“two”) + pang- -an → pangdalawahan (“for two”) matagal (“long (of a period of time)”) + pang- -an → pangmatagalan (“for long-term”)
Usage notes[edit]
Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. bili + pang- -an → pamilihan dalawa + pang- -an → pangdalawahan
Derived terms[edit]
Tagalog terms circumfixed with pang- -an pamahiran pamayanan panindigan pangatawanan panghihinawan panghimasukan pangmatagalan pinanggalingan
Anagrams[edit]
pag- -nan

sa- -nan.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
sa- -an
sa- -han
See sa- and -nan.

pang- -han.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
pam- -han — before root initials ⟨b⟩ and ⟨p⟩, will sometimes remove initial ⟨b⟩ and ⟨p⟩
pan- -han — before root initials ⟨d⟩, ⟨s⟩ and ⟨t⟩, will sometimes remove initial ⟨d⟩, ⟨s⟩ and ⟨t⟩
pang- -han.function: alternative form of pang- -an; Normally, /h/ is inserted before -an when the root word ends with a vowel that is not followed by a glottal stop. bili + pang- -han → pamilihan anino + pang- -han → panganinuhan dalawa + pang- -han → pangdalawahan

sang- -han.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
sang- -an
san- -an
See sang- and -han.
sang- -han.function: alternative form of sang- -an sang- -han + tao (“human”) → sangkatauhan (“humankind”)

sang- -an.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
sang- -han
san- -an
See sang- and -an.
sang- -an.function: forms nouns to denote a larger whole sang- -an + guni (“mind”) → sanggunian (“reference; council”)

san- -an.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
sang- -an
sang- -han
See san- and -an.
san- -an.function: alternative form of sang- -an san- -an + libot (“wandering”) → sanlibutan (“whole world; universe”) san- -an + daigdig (“world”) → sandaigdigan (“the whole world”) san- -an + sukob (“together in the same refuge”) + -in- → sansinukuban (“universe”)
